sourceReferences.locator: PDF pages 11, 27, 37

contentAllocationReferences.locator: Official specification PDF pages 8–9, Qualification at a glance
contentAllocationReferences.evidenceSummary: Paper 1 assesses Pure Mathematics; Paper 2 assesses Statistics and Mechanics.

sourceReferences.locator: page 8
sourceReferences.evidenceSummary: Qualification at a glance: Paper 1: Pure Mathematics (*Paper code: 8MA0/01), written examination: 2 hours, 62.5% of the qualification, 100 marks. Content overview: Topic 1 – Proof, Topic 2 – Algebra and functions, Topic 3 – Coordinate geometry, Topic 4 – Sequences and series, Topic 5 – Trigonometry, Topic 6 – Exponentials and logarithms, Topic 7 – Differentiation, Topic 8 – Integration, Topic 9 – Vectors.

sourceReferences.locator: page 9
sourceReferences.evidenceSummary: Paper 2: Statistics and Mechanics (*Paper code: 8MA0/02), written examination: 1 hour 15 minutes, 37.5% of the qualification, 60 marks. Content overview: Section A: Statistics (Topic 1 – Statistical sampling, Topic 2 – Data presentation and interpretation, Topic 3 – Probability, Topic 4 – Statistical distributions, Topic 5 – Statistical hypothesis testing); Section B: Mechanics (Topic 6 – Quantities and units in mechanics, Topic 7 – Kinematics, Topic 8 – Forces and Newton’s laws).

routes.name: AS Mathematics
routes.selectionRule: all

sourceReferences.locator: page 8

contentAllocationReferences.locator: Official specification PDF pages 4 and 72–74, assessment overview

sourceReferences.locator: OFFICIAL PDF PAGE 4

sourceReferences.locator: OFFICIAL PDF PAGE 78

sourceReferences.locator: OFFICIAL PDF PAGE 4
sourceReferences.evidenceSummary: Paper 2: Pure Mathematics and Statistics (02), 100 marks, 2 hour written paper, 33⅓% of total A Level. Two sections: Pure Mathematics (~50 marks) and Statistics (~50 marks).

sourceReferences.locator: OFFICIAL PDF PAGE 73
sourceReferences.evidenceSummary: Paper 2 description: assesses Pure Mathematics and Statistics, structured in two sections of ~50 marks each; some questions set in context of pre-release large data set.

sourceReferences.locator: OFFICIAL PDF PAGE 78
sourceReferences.evidenceSummary: Entry code table lists Component 02 Pure Mathematics and Statistics as external assessment.

sourceReferences.locator: OFFICIAL PDF PAGE 4
sourceReferences.evidenceSummary: Paper 3: Pure Mathematics and Mechanics (03), 100 marks, 2 hour written paper, 33⅓% of total A Level. Two sections: Pure Mathematics (~50 marks) and Mechanics (~50 marks).

sourceReferences.locator: OFFICIAL PDF PAGE 74
sourceReferences.evidenceSummary: Paper 3 description: assesses Pure Mathematics and Mechanics, structured in two sections of ~50 marks each.

sourceReferences.locator: OFFICIAL PDF PAGE 78
sourceReferences.evidenceSummary: Entry code table lists Component 03 Pure Mathematics and Mechanics as external assessment.

routes.name: A Level Further Mathematics A
routes.selectionRule: Mandatory: Y540, Y541. Optional: choose at least two papers from Y542, Y543, Y544, Y545. If more than two optional papers are taken, the best two results (by grade) are used to determine the qualification grade.

Page 89: 'consists of two mandatory papers and two optional papers'. Page 94 table with entry codes and description of optional paper flexibility.

routes.name: GCSE Mathematics and Numeracy (Double Award)
routes.selectionRule: Candidates must take exactly one paper from each unit group: {UNIT1H, UNIT1F}, {UNIT2H, UNIT2F}, {UNIT3H, UNIT3F}. All three selected papers are mandatory. Tiers may be mixed across units.

sourceReferences.locator: PDF pages 5, 6, 59
sourceReferences.evidenceSummary: The qualification comprises three mandatory units. Each unit is offered at Higher and Foundation tier. Candidates may be entered at different tiers across units. All units must be completed to obtain the award.
